description.en: Parameters: fullcircle – a number Set angle measurement units, i.e. set number of “degrees” for a full circle. Default value is 360 degrees. >>> turtle.home() >>> turtle.left(90) >>> turtle.heading() 90.0 >>> # Change angle measurement unit to grad (also known as gon, >>> # grade, or gradian and equals 1/100-th of the right angle.) >>> turtle.degrees(400.0) >>> turtle.heading() 100.0 >>> turtle.degrees(360) >>> turtle.heading() 90.0

excerpt.es: Parameters: fullcircle – a number Set angle measurement units, i.e. set number of “degrees” for a full circle. Default value is 360 degrees. >>> turtle.home() >>> turtle.left(90) >>> turtle.heading() ...
excerpt.en: Parameters: fullcircle – a number Set angle measurement units, i.e. set number of “degrees” for a full circle. Default value is 360 degrees. >>> turtle.home() >>> turtle.left(90) >>> turtle.heading() ...

description.en: Parameters: delay – positive integer Set or return the drawing delay in milliseconds. (This is approximately the time interval between two consecutive canvas updates.) The longer the drawing delay, the slower the animation. Optional argument: >>> screen.delay() 10 >>> screen.delay(5) >>> screen.delay() 5

description.en: Parameters: x – a number or a pair/vector of numbers or a turtle instance y – a number if x is a number, else None Return the distance from the turtle to (x,y), the given vector, or the given other turtle, in turtle step units. >>> turtle.home() >>> turtle.distance(30,40) 50.0 >>> turtle.distance((30,40)) 50.0 >>> joe = Turtle() >>> joe.forward(77) >>> turtle.distance(joe) 77.0

excerpt.es: Parameters: x – a number or a pair/vector of numbers or a turtle instance y – a number if x is a number, else None Return the distance from the turtle to (x,y), the given vector, or the given other tu...

description.en: Parameters: size – an integer >= 1 (if given) color – a colorstring or a numeric color tuple Draw a circular dot with diameter size, using color. If size is not given, the maximum of pensize+4 and 2*pensize is used. >>> turtle.home() >>> turtle.dot() >>> turtle.fd(50); turtle.dot(20, "blue"); turtle.fd(50) >>> turtle.position() (100.00,-0.00) >>> turtle.heading() 0.0
